review.reviewer: Codex independent static review agent r015_parser_review

claims.description: PRD v2 frontmatter is decoded by the YAML document parser with strict mode, unique keys, the core schema and aliases disabled during conversion.
Normative Markdown structure is selected and validated from typed mdast/CommonMark nodes, including the Vertical slices heading and its single unordered list.
Regular expressions in the PRD v2 task-header path operate on scalar text already selected from AST leaf nodes; leaf coercion and diagnostics remain inside the leaf parser boundary.
The mutable three-character task marker is located from the AST source position and revalidated against the exact source slice before its location is exposed.
Broad line-oriented regular expressions belong to the separate classic compatibility adapter and are not imported into the PRD v2 parser.

evidenceTrail.action: Bind the review to the exact parser source snapshot
evidenceTrail.result: SHA-256, byte count and line count recorded

evidenceTrail.action: Inspect YAML and Markdown parser entry boundaries
evidenceTrail.result: Safe YAML settings and CommonMark/mdast structural selection confirmed

evidenceTrail.action: Trace every structural regular-expression use in the PRD v2 task path
evidenceTrail.result: Uses are limited to AST-selected scalar leaf text and the bounded three-character marker
Line-oriented classic parsing remains isolated in packages/prd/src/classic.ts

limitations: This receipt attests to the exact source snapshot identified above; any parser source change invalidates the binding until a new independent review is recorded.
The review was static. It did not execute Bun, tests, builds, the CLI or the TUI.
conclusion: APPROVED: the reviewed PRD v2 parser uses YAML and CommonMark/mdast parsing for document structure, confines regular expressions to leaf/scalar validation or an exact three-character marker, and keeps classic line-oriented parsing in a separate adapter.
